channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge in Seattle on Tuesday blocked President Donald Trump’s suspension of the nation’s refugee admissions system, saying that while the president has broad authority over who comes into the country, he cannot nullify the law passed by Congress establishing the program.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: He's had three 'last meals' on Oklahoma's death row. The Supreme Court has now tossed his conviction
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Richard Glossip has spent 27 years behind bars, most of it on Oklahoma's death row, coming close enough to execution that he has had nine separate execution dates and been fed three “last meals.” On Tuesday, Glossip, now 62, won a new trial in a stunning U.S.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nashville hot chicken up for official state food in Tennessee legislature
item.description: Nashville hot chicken traces its origins back to the 1930s, and popularity of the dish has grown to international acclaim.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: CARFAX expert tells you what to know before purchasing a car
item.description: Carfax's Editor-in-Chief Patrick Olsen reports that the agency monitors vehicle prices across seven categories.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Joyce, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration closes Office of Transformation
item.description: The Office of Transformation, according to its website, is responsible for “strategic guidance and oversight of enterprise-wide initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: As DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor, here's what to know about Casey
item.description: The aspirations of Florida’s first lady Casey DeSantis have long been a topic of discussion in Tallahassee.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Missouri woman pleads guilty to federal charge in plot to sell Graceland
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A Missouri woman pleaded guilty Tuesday to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma State Department of Education seeks supplier of Bible lessons
item.description: The State Department of Education is now looking for suppliers to get Bible lessons in Oklahoma elementary school curriculum.
item.dc:creator: Austin Breasette/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines flight forced to make emergency landing at Newark
item.description: A United Airlines flight carrying nearly 200 passengers made an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport after a "possible mechanical issue" mid-flight, the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jon Stewart cuts hand during fiery monologue on DOGE, prescription drugs
item.description: Jon Stewart is railing against sweeping cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — and giving his hand one of its own in the process — during an impassioned monologue on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Husband of Ruby Franke, Utah mommy blogger convicted of child abuse, has 'regrets' after wife's fall from fame
item.description: Utah parenting blogger Ruby Franke's husband says he has 'regrets' after his wife's descent into chaos. Ruby pleaded guilty to abusing two of her children in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: New York inmate dies in prison as guards continue strike deemed illegal under state law
item.description: An inmate died in a New York prison as guards at prisons across the state continue their strike, which union officials acknowledge violates a state law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Catholic community in Utah 'united in prayer' for the health of Pope Francis
item.description: The pope showed some slight improvements in recent tests, and as he continues to recover, members of the Catholic faith around the world — including in Utah — continue to pray for his health.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secretive US Space Force plane shares rare photo of Earth from orbit
item.description: The United States Space Force shared a never-before-seen glimpse into its notably secretive X-37B space plane in orbit. The photo showed the space plane and Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mississippi city drops lawsuit over newspaper editorial that judge ordered removed
item.description: A Mississippi city dropped its lawsuit Monday against a newspaper that had its editorial criticizing local leaders removed by a judge in a case that sparked widespread outrage from First Amendment advocates.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump expressed hope that Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he met Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the invasion.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer shot and killed while responding to a hospital intensive care unit where authorities said a gunman had shot three hospital employees and taken staff members hostage last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court declines to take up challenge to Tennessee law restricting drag shows
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a challenge to a Tennessee law restricting some drag performances, allowing the first-in-the-nation law to remain largely intact.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A wildcat strike by guards at New York's prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court schedules arguments in case where Trump administration is defending ACA
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday scheduled arguments for April 21 in a case that could decide the legality of the Affordable Care Act’s (ACA) requirement that insurers cover certain preventive services.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Students at elite NYC college expelled for disrupting class with threatening Hamas propaganda
item.description: Two Barnard College students have been expelled for their roles in disrupting a Jan. 21 history of modern Israel class, according to social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Rodriguez makes half-court shot, wins Bucknell student $10,000
item.description: Former Major League Baseball star Alex Rodriguez showed his skill on the hardwood Sunday, making a half-court shot to win a Bucknell student $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, Fox 5

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group was buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery in Minnesota. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
